content: {"perspective": "Regulatory", "question": "Rounds 1-2 established that (a) no regulator has issued a formal IF therapeutic opinion, and (b) commercial IF apps market weight-loss and fat-burn benefits without facing enforcement. Now consider the AHA 2024 conference abstract signal linking 8-hour eating windows to 91% higher cardiovascular death risk and the FTC/FDA posture on preliminary evidence: would an unpublished, non-peer-reviewed conference abstract be sufficient evidentiary basis for an FTC substantiation challenge or an FDA safety communication about IF apps — or does the regulatory framework explicitly exclude conference abstracts from both directions (supporting and refuting product claims)? What precedents exist for regulators acting on pre-peer-review safety signals in the dietary supplement, medical device, or digital health space?", "search_queries": ["FTC substantiation standard conference abstract preliminary evidence health claim enforcement precedent", "FDA safety communication dietary supplement conference abstract preliminary data withdrawal warning", "AHA 2024 time-restricted eating 91% cardiovascular death FTC FDA regulatory response statement", "FDA digital health app wellness tracker regulatory precedent safety signal pre-publication", "FTC competent reliable scientific evidence standard health claim dietary intervention preliminary"]}
